title: PM praises Australia social ban for U-16s, hinting at similar curbs
content: India is considering stricter age limits for social media access. Prime Minister Modi praised Australia's ban on platforms for children under sixteen. This move follows growing government focus on online safety for minors. Several Indian states have also proposed similar restrictions on social media use. Nationwide implementation faces significant challenges due to India's vast internet user population.

title: FIFA World Cup: Merino magic helps Spain edge Belgium, set up France semifinal
content: Mikel Merino scored a late winner to secure Spain's World Cup semi-final spot. Spain defeated Belgium 2-1 in a thrilling quarter-final match on Friday. They will now face tournament favorites France in their next game. Spain extended their impressive unbeaten run to thirty-seven matches. Belgium's eighteen-game undefeated streak concluded with this loss.

title: Sinner beats Djokovic in straight sets, sets up Wimbledon final clash against Zverev
content: Jannik Sinner stormed into the Wimbledon final with a straight-sets win over Novak Djokovic and will defend his title against Alexander Zverev. The German reached his maiden Wimbledon final after beating Arthur Fery, saying he believes he can win. Zverev also praised Fery's potential and applauded the Wimbledon crowd for its fair and electric support.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use. He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption. The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings. Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave. This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: Hours after return to Twitter, Zuckerberg gets business proposal from product head
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X.  X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account.  This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement.  The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks.  Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: One Walmart heiress owns $300M yacht, another has a giant Texas ranch
content: Walmart heiresses Ann Walton Kroenke and Nancy Walton Laurie are icons of immense wealth, each showcasing their lifestyle choices through extravagant assets. While Ann is the proud owner of the sprawling Waggoner Ranch, one of the largest in the U.S., Nancy indulges in her luxurious $300 million superyacht, Kaos, which has drawn the ire of environmental activists.

title: Scientists thickened Arctic ice with seawater, but there is a big catch
content: Scientists tested spraying seawater onto Arctic ice, which showed promising initial results. The treated ice became thicker and more reflective, resisting summer melting effectively. This method offers an alternative to controversial geoengineering approaches for ice preservation. While promising, researchers note significant challenges for large-scale implementation across the Arctic. Further work is essential before this technique can be considered for broader application.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Breast cancer survival rate up, but India lags behind rich nations: WHO
content: India's breast cancer survival rate stands at sixty-five point seven percent. This figure is lower than the global median survival rate. Early diagnosis and timely treatment are crucial for improving survival outcomes. Disparities exist in access to quality cancer care across regions. Strengthening detection and treatment access is essential for better results.

title: Andy Burnham poised to be next UK PM
content: Andy Burnham is expected to become Labour leader next week.  He will then take office as Britain’s new prime minister on July 20.  Burnham secured significant backing from Labour MPs for his leadership bid.  He will replace Keir Starmer, who is expected to resign that day.  Burnham aims to offer a new political approach for the country.

title: Follow 'correct script, diction' of national song, anthem: Centre
content: The government has instructed all states to strictly follow correct script and diction. State songs must now be played before the national song and anthem. This directive ensures proper adherence to national symbols during official functions. The home ministry provided the correct script and pronunciation details on its portal. States are requested to issue suitable instructions for strict compliance by all organizations.

title: Who are Lamine Yamal's parents? Meet Mounir Nasraoui and Sheila Ebana
content: Lamine Yamal's parents, Mounir Nasraoui from Morocco and Sheila Ebana from Equatorial Guinea, both immigrated to Spain and raised Yamal in the greater Barcelona area under modest financial conditions. After separating when Yamal was around three years old, his mother raised him in Rocafonda, Mataró, while working to make ends meet. His name honors two men who helped his family during a period of hardship. Despite his Spanish nationality and international football career with La Roja, Yamal proudly represents his Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean heritage, wearing his parents' national flags on his boots at every match.

title: Zohran Mamdani defends Taylor Swift wedding amid $160K security row
content: Taylor Swift covered the $160,000 police permit and security costs for her wedding. Mayor Zohran Mamdani confirmed Swift paid the city for the event. This payment addressed Republican concerns about taxpayer funding for the celebration. The wedding required extensive security measures usually reserved for heads of state. Swift's payment settled the political debate surrounding the event's expenses.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Pittsburgh Penguins emerge as potential landing spot for $2.6M Seattle Kraken's young center
content: The Seattle Kraken’s young center Shane Wright is looking to move on from the team after an average performance on the ice in the last season. With just 12 goals and 27 points across 74 games in the last season, Shane Wright is looking to sign with a new team. Amid all the mess, the Pittsburgh Penguins have emerged as a potential landing spot for the star player.

title: Can't block calls from 1600 no. series, but can put 140 series on DND: Trai
content: Regulated entities and government communications from 1600 numbers cannot be blocked by any application. Promotional calls from the 140 series can be blocked using the DND registry. Trai mandates 1600 numbers for trustworthy service and transaction calls to existing customers. Registered telemarketers use the 140 number series for promotional communications. Customers can register preferences on the DND registry to allow or block these promotional calls.

title: SC: Even a snail can question pace of trials
content: The Supreme Court criticized a commercial suit's slow progress since 2015.  Plaintiff's evidence is still ongoing, prompting a strong judicial observation.  The court rejected Levitate Mobile Technologies' plea for additional documents.  This plea was similar to a previous one that was also denied.  The court noted the significant delay in the judicial process.

title: Erling Haaland's birthday surprise for bullied fan wins hearts worldwide
content: Erling Haaland surprised a young Brazilian fan with a birthday video after the boy was bullied for growing his hair like the Manchester City star. In the heartfelt message, Haaland praised his hairstyle, encouraged him to ignore negative comments and wished him a happy birthday. The video, shared by Barstool Sports, has touched football fans around the world.

title: Truce and fire: The ghost of Versailles haunts Trump. But is Iran overplaying its hand?
content: President Trump declared the June 17 ceasefire with Iran over, ordering fresh US strikes. Iran responded by targeting tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions. The conflict's tempo is now largely controlled by Iran's ability to disrupt oil shipments. Both sides face pressures, with neither in a decisive position to end the war. Managed instability with periodic attacks and negotiations is the likely immediate outcome.

title: Women activists: Govt must implement 33% women’s quota at current strength of Parliament
content: Women's groups demand immediate 33% reservation in legislatures and Parliament. They seek a constitutional amendment to delink reservation from census and delimitation. Activists are urging MPs to support this crucial demand for political representation. Joint demonstrations are planned across the country on July 20-21. This push aims to fulfill long-standing calls for women's empowerment.

title: Reverse oil flow: India supplies gasoline to Russia - why it matters
content: Indian refiners are well equipped from a product-quality standpoint to supply the gasoline grades required by Russia. However, Oil minister Hardeep Singh Puri has said that Indian companies are not directly selling gasoline to Russia.

title: Game over? Xbox layoffs by Indian-American CEO Asha Sharma ignites H-1B furor
content: Microsoft's Xbox division announced significant layoffs and studio transfers for fiscal year 2027.  These cuts are part of a major corporate restructuring aimed at improving business health.  Misinformation quickly spread online, falsely accusing an Indian-origin executive of targeting American workers.  Federal data does not support claims of widespread H-1B visa abuse by the company.  The controversy highlights ongoing debates about immigration and the American workforce.

title: Pak refuses to accept grooming gang leader unless UK hands over political dissidents
content: Pakistan demands UK extradition of dissidents for deporting Shabir Ahmed.  Ahmed, a grooming gang leader, faces deportation after his British citizenship was revoked.  Pakistan states it will not accept him without reciprocal extradition agreements.  Islamabad wants UK extradition of figures like Shahzad Akbar and Adil Raja.  This creates a stalemate, with the UK unlikely to extradite dissidents.

title: 'Ronaldo will never be a problem': New Portugal coach Jesus
content: Portugal have appointed Jorge Jesus as head coach following their World Cup exit, replacing Roberto Martinez. Backing Cristiano Ronaldo amid criticism, Jesus said the veteran "will never be a problem" and called him "a symbol of Portuguese football." The 71-year-old, who recently coached Ronaldo at Al Nassr, now begins rebuilding Portugal ahead of future international tournaments.

title: 'Bomb them like never before': Trump says 'left instructions' to strike Iran if assassinated
content: President Donald Trump stated he has ordered a massive military response if Iran attempts to assassinate him.  He dismissed reports of new Israeli intelligence regarding a specific Iranian plot.  Iran has long sought retaliation for the killing of Qassem Soleimani.  US officials believe Israeli intelligence may influence military action decisions.  Despite tensions, Qatar remains committed to pursuing diplomatic efforts between nations.

title: NIA chargesheets Hurriyat’s Shah, 5 others in 1996 mob violence case
content: The NIA filed a chargesheet against six Hurriyat Conference leaders for 1996 violence.  These charges include criminal conspiracy and attempt to murder during a militant's funeral.  The agency claims the violence was part of a larger separatist conspiracy.  The case was transferred to the NIA from J&K Police in April.  A Jammu court rejected Shabir Ahmad Shah's bail plea last week.

title: After Farooq’s protest invite, Mirwaiz calls for Art 370 inclusion; BJP shreds NC
content: Farooq Abdullah invited politicians for a protest demanding Jammu and Kashmir statehood restoration. Mirwaiz Umar Farooq suggested broadening the protest's agenda for political resolution. The BJP rejected the invitation, criticizing the National Conference's past actions. The National Conference is still awaiting permission for the planned protest. Political reactions highlight divisions over the region's future.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for July 11, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 11, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Monsoon session: NDA in command, opposition in disarray. Will DMK tilt the balance?
content: The opposition parties are grappling with significant turmoil following vital splits that have diminished their strength in Parliament. Recent shifts have bolstered the ruling NDA coalition's standing. As the Monsoon session approaches, the potential reintroduction of a Constitution Amendment Bill looms. Internal fractures within several regional parties could further compromise the opposition's capacity to impede crucial legislation.

title: How much prize money did Arthur Fery win after semi-final defeat to Zverev?
content: Arthur Fery's dream Wimbledon run ended with a semifinal defeat to Alexander Zverev, but the 23-year-old British wildcard enjoyed a career-defining tournament. Reaching the last four more than doubled his previous career prize money of around £650,000 and is set to boost his ATP ranking. Zverev praised Fery, saying he is destined to achieve "amazing things" in tennis.

title: 'Hang me in front of temple': Ex-Siddhivinayak trust chief rejects Shinde's donation 'loot' charge
content: Maharashtra deputy CM Eknath Shinde alleged donation irregularities at Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple. He questioned the silence on alleged temple donation looting during the previous regime. Former Siddhivinayak Trust chairman Aadesh Bandekar rejected these allegations and demanded a fair inquiry. A controversy erupted in June over alleged irregularities in Ram Temple donation counting. An SIT found evidence of embezzlement, leading to arrests at the Ayodhya temple.

title: Jason Kelce shares heartfelt reaction to Travis Kelce-Taylor Swift wedding
content: Jason Kelce shared his enjoyment of his brother Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift’s wedding festivities. He admitted to consuming a significant number of beers during the celebratory royal event. The couple recently celebrated their union with a honeymoon trip to Montana. They were spotted with Jason Kelce and his wife Kylie Kelce there. Travis and Taylor are now heading to California for a teammate's wedding.

title: Indian Railways reservation rules every traveller should know to avoid waitlisted tickets
content: Indian Railways has reduced advance train ticket bookings to sixty days. General quota bookings now open daily at eight AM for passengers. Tatkal bookings commence one day before travel for AC and non-AC classes. Aadhaar verification is mandatory for genuine passengers seeking confirmed tickets. These updated rules aim to improve ticket booking transparency and accessibility.

title: FE - Lucas Di Grassi takes Formula E GEN1 up the hill at Goodwood Festival of Speed
content: Lola Yamaha ABT driver Lucas Di Grassi drove the Formula E GEN1 car at the Goodwood Festival of Speed on Thursday. He said he made history in Formula E as he drove the first-generation car at the event where all four generations of cars are being put on display for motorsport fans. The festival will continue until Sunday.

title: Man falls into waterlogged Noida drain, dies
content: A 28-year-old man died after falling into a rainwater-filled drain in Noida's Sector 58. He was walking on unstable concrete slabs over the drain amid heavy waterlogging. His friend and others pulled him out unconscious, but he could not be revived. This incident follows other rain-related accidents in Gautam Buddh Nagar district. Concerns over uncovered drains and civic safety have been highlighted by these events.

title: E20 petrol row: Govt answers 9 FAQs on mileage, performance, damage, pricing & more
content: The government has said that the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme is a major component of India's energy strategy, designed to strengthen energy security, support the farming community and lower environmental impact by increasing the use of domestically produced renewable fuel.

title: India building strategic oil reserves: ONGC approves 1.75-million-tonne storage capacity
content: As the world's third-largest importer and consumer of crude oil, India relies on imports for more than 88 per cent of its crude oil requirements. Against the backdrop of geopolitical uncertainty and fluctuations in global oil markets.

title: Watch: Sachin, hailed as 'perhaps the greatest batsman of all time', spotted at Wimbledon
content: Sachin Tendulkar attended the Wimbledon men's singles semifinals on Friday, where commentators hailed him as "perhaps the greatest batsman of all time." The cricket legend watched British wild card Arthur Fery face French Open champion Alexander Zverev on Centre Court. A regular Wimbledon visitor, Tendulkar once again drew attention as one of the event's high-profile guests.

title: Delhi High Court orders Rajpal Yadav’s imprisonment in ₹9 crore debt case
content: The Delhi High Court upheld actor Rajpal Yadav's conviction and sentence in multiple cheque dishonour cases.  The court dismissed 21 petitions filed by the actor and his wife, Radha Rajpal Yadav.  Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma noted the couple's repeated failure to fulfill financial commitments.  The actor's conduct, including stating he would go to jail rather than repay, was criticized.  The court affirmed the Sessions Court's judgment, dismissing all related applications.

title: 'Lost my son & dad': Ketan's father writes to President, seeks fast-track justice
content: Vishal Agarwal has appealed to President Murmu for a swift investigation. He seeks a fast-track court hearing for his son Ketan's murder case. The family suffered immense grief after losing both son and father recently. Ketan was allegedly murdered by his fiancée and her friend. The investigation into this tragic incident is currently ongoing.

title: 'One nation, one election' mechanism may be ready for rollout by 2029: Joint panel chief
content: P.P. Chaudhary claimed that nearly 99 % of civil society stakeholders consulted so far have backed the proposal

title: AAP to launch signature campaign for action in Ram Temple donation row
content: As part of the campaign, a letter addressed to Prime Minister Narendra Modi will be signed by people across India.

title: First batch of Kailash-Mansarovar Yatra pilgrims reaches Tibet
content: Officials from the Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam (KMVN) – the nodal agency for the yatra – stationed at the Dharchula base camp said the 48-member batch crossed the Lipulekh Pass at 9.05 a.m.

title: CPI questions PM Modi’s silence over Ram Temple case
content: Union government ‘betrayed trust of the people’, resignations alone would not suffice, says party general secretary Raja

title: Phone calls from 1600 number series cannot be blocked, DND app can block calls from 140 series: TRAI
content: The key objective of assigning designated series for these important communications is to make such calls trustworthy for the citizens, the regulator said

title: Civil rights organisations demand probe into Baruipur encounter
content: Representatives of civil rights group have raised questions over the police’s version of events leading to the encounter and sought an investigation by a judge

title: ₹3,800-crore assets seized in Mahadev betting app case: ED
content: The agency said its Raipur Zonal Office attached movable and immovable properties worth ₹941 crore belonging to businessman Vikas Garg and his family members

title: Man hurls papers at Supreme Court Bench after incoherent submissions
content: Litigant escorted out after disruption in court; court decides not to take action despite ‘unparliamentary utterances’

title: As National Conference canvasses for J&K Statehood protest, BJP announces stir
content: NC leader Tanvir Sadiq says all leaders should rise above party lines and stand united for the people of Jammu & Kashmir at the July 20 protest; BJP announces its own agenda with protests on employment issue on the same day

title: PM’s New Zealand visit will unlock new avenues of cooperation: govt.
content: During his two-day visit, PM Modi will meet with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and other leaders, focusing on enhancing economic and trade relations

title: NIA files charge sheet against six separatist leaders in 1996 Kashmir mob violence case
content: The six were charged under provisions of the Ranbir Penal Code, 1989, for criminal conspiracy, attempt to murder, rioting and assault on public servants, along with Section 13 of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967

title: Police freeze 12 more bank accounts of Trinamool with over ₹1,000 crore
content: Action follows complaints of illegal diversion of funds from the accounts; ED had earlier frozen party’s three accounts with ₹440 crore; High Court to take up case on Monday; Rajya Sabha MP slams ED action, asks when it will freeze temple funds

title: ‘Operation Toofan’: police step up vigil on drivers
content: According to police sources, the recent rise in cases involving synthetic drug use among some transport workers has raised concerns about passenger safety

title: Demolition of tilted building on M.G. Road deferred
content: The decision followed advice from expert engaged by the Corporation, who warned that demolition without precautionary measures could endanger nearby buildings and workers

title: Vijay announces memorial for Karur stampede victims, faults police for not informing him of the crowd
content: Speaking at a public meeting held on the outskirts of Karur, he says the tragedy could have been averted had the police alerted him to the gathering during his election campaign at Velusamypuram on September 27, 2025

title: IITs, IIMs push back against VBSA norms
content: Institutes want guarantees on autonomy, clarity on applicability of statutory provisions governing them; “differential and unintended” consequences of penalty system also flagged

title: Hit-and-run twist in fatal accident in Kerala capital; car driver nabbed based on CCTV evidence
content: Police apprehend car driver after establishing that the motorcycle was hit from behind before the four-wheeler sped away from the scene in Thiruvallam. Two young IT professionals were killed in the incident

title: The uncertain flight of Parandur airport
content: The proposed Parandur airport project in Tamil Nadu has been put on hold, though there is no official announcement yet. While the protesting residents of Ekanapuram, the village that falls within both the proposed runways, are happy that the government plans to drop the project, residents of other villages remain uncertain about their future. Sunitha S. reports on the challenges surrounding a project that has left many frustrated

title: Kerala govt. forms three-member panel for debris removal at Kalladi tunnel site in Wayanad
content: The decision was taken at a meeting attended by Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan, Public Works Minister P.K. Basheer and Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala on July 10

title: Telangana faces dry conditions with significant rainfall unlikely in July
content: Significant rainfall is likely only if weather systems develop closer to the Andhra Pradesh coast, says Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) scientist

title: Headmistress suspended for screening Chief Minister’s function at government school in Karur
content: Stating it is improper to screen the live broadcast of a TVK event on the school premises, Minister Rajmohan asserts that there is no place for politics in educational institutions

title: As Kashmir varsities cull ‘objectionable’ books, fear of losing academic rigour grows among scholars
content: Political leaders, scholars and varsity professors object to the move citing self-censorship and interference of the state; order comes days after a book, that profiled Kashmiri separatist leaders, was circulated in J&K schools under the national Samagra Shiksha scheme.

title: Malayalam gets a cosy space in Australian public library
content: The Max Webber Public Library in the Sydney suburbs has opened a Malayalam section featuring 450 titles, following consistent demand from the diaspora. Malayalam has become the seventh Indian language to be represented in the library. Keralites there hope the initiative will help them connect with their culture and language better

title: Catholic body meets Amit Shah, flags concerns over FCRA Bill
content: The new Rules bar proselytisation; CBCI said inclusion of such words “amounts to degrading the good intentioned charitable, educational, healthcare, and humanitarian services rendered by faith-based organisations”

title: AAP asks PM to rise above ‘partisan’ politics over attacks on churches
content: AAP MP Sanjay Singh urges the Prime Minister to prevent the misuse of anti-conversion laws to harass members of the community

title: Bihar BJP candidate withdraws his candidature from Bankipur bypoll
content: Party insiders says Abhishek Kumar alias Bunty was considered a weak candidate against Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor; candidate withdraws citing family reasons

title: Supreme Court pulls up Maharashtra govt. over ‘disturbing’ delays in criminal trials
content: Court directs government to file an affidavit explaining reasons for the prolonged pendency of trial and whether it has a policy to prevent accused persons from remaining in custody due to institutional limitations

title: Expert panel against approving ₹10,000 crore Sharavathi Pumped Storage Project citing irreversible ecological harm
content: Project does not contribute additional electricity but will cause net loss of 500 MW, said the report submitted to the High Court

title: Four alleged gangsters killed in gunfight with police in Gurugram
content: The gang was planning to target the house of businessman to whom they had made several extortion calls, say police; one gang member critically injured, three police personnel wounded

title: Bihar govt. to dismiss over 3,000 school teachers for using fake degrees, forged documents to secure jobs
content: The government would also initiate criminal action and recover the salaries paid to them during their service period

title: APTA urges teachers, employees to join FAPTO protest on July 14
content: Association calls for large-scale participation in rallies and Collectorate picketing across Andhra Pradesh, demanding resolution of long-pending financial issues and implementation of PRC

title: Flood-Like Situation In Jammu And Kashmir After Heavy Rainfall, 25 Rescued
content: The water levels rose sharply in rivers, streams and low-lying areas as intense rain triggered a flood-like situation in Mendhar sub-division of Poonch district of Jammu and Kashmir on Friday.

title: US Targets Dubai-Based "Key Financier" For Mojtaba Khamenei
content: Iranian national Ali Ansari is described in a US Treasury statement as a "key financier" for the Islamic republic's supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei.

title: US Court Delays Decision On Murder Trial Over Charlie Kirk Killing
content: A US judge said Friday the preliminary hearing into the killing of right-wing influencer Charlie Kirk will be taken up again in September, delaying a decision on whether there is enough evidence to hold a murder trial.

title: Highlights: Heavy Rain Alert For Delhi, UP, Monsoon Covers Entire Country
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Southwest Monsoon causes heavy rain and waterlogging in Delhi NCR and Uttar Pradesh

title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Two Former Employees For Trade Secrets Theft
content: The complaint, filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of California, alleges a coordinated effort to steal Apple's confidential information, including product designs, manufacturing processes and supply chain strategies.

title: Iran Must End Hormuz Ship Attacks, Keep Lanes Open: US Officials
content: Iran has told Washington that recent attacks on shipping in the Strait were from "an errant part of their system," one senior official said.

title: Ukraine Creates "Long-Range" Command To Step Up Strikes On Russia
content: In recent weeks, Ukraine has reported strikes almost daily, with officials saying it is only fair to bring the war to Russia more than four years since Moscow launched its full-scale invasion.

title: Prince Harry And Family Meet King Charles For First Time In 4 Years: Report
content: Harry arrived in the UK on Monday in a visit to mark the one-year countdown to next year's Invictus Games for wounded veterans, which he founded.

title: US Senators Announce Trump Deal On Russia Sanctions Bill
content: The measure has been under negotiation for months but has struggled to advance amid a lukewarm response from the White House and Trump's shifting approach toward Russian President Vladimir Putin.

title: "Bomb Them": Trump Says He's Left Instructions If Iran Assassinates Him
content: When asked about the new plot by Iran to assassinate him that Israel warned about, US President Donald Trump said that there were no new plans. However, he claimed that Iran has wanted him dead for years.

title: 'Amaira Gave Distress Signal': Jaipur Couple Recalls Daughter's Final Hours
content: Speaking to NDTV, the Jaipur couple described the latest footage as a minute-by-minute account of what they believe was an episode of bullying, repeated appeals for help, and a series of missed opportunities.

title: Assam's Rs 2,100-Crore Plan To Build Satellite City Around Guwahati Airport
content: The project, based on the Aerotropolis model, aims to turn the area surrounding the airport into a major centre for business, industry and urban development.

title: Aviation Watchdog Warns IndiGo Over Goods Handling Lapses
content: Based on the findings, DGCA said the airline has been asked to review the training programme to include more practical exercises and operational scenarios.

title: In Assam Budget, 'Green Cess' Proposal To Fund Environmental Projects
content: According to the budget proposals, the revenue generated from the cess will be used for afforestation, pollution control, biodiversity conservation, climate adaptation, promotion of green energy and development of sustainable infrastructure

title: BJP Replaces Candidate Against Prashant Kishor For Bypoll In Bihar's Bankipur
content: Abhishek Kumar Sinha, popularly known as Bunty, who is a vice-president of the Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha's state unit, expressed his inability to contest the bypoll a day after filing nomination papers.

title: 'Playing With Fire': Minister To NDTV On Satluj Screenings By Akali Dal
content: Union Minister Ravneet Singh Bittu has launched a strongly-worded attack on 'Satluj' filmmakers, accusing the lead actor, Diljit Dosanjh, and director Honey Trehan of spreading a dangerous narrative and playing with fire

title: "Teacher Pretended Nobody Was Missing": Parents Of Girl Who Jumped To Death
content: Shivani Meena underlined that her daughter was very happy when she arrived at the school that November morning.

title: 'Satluj Row Only Amplified Curiosity': Journalist Who Broke Khalra Murder Story
content: 'Satluj', which dramatises the extra-judicial killing of Jaswant Singh Khalra by the Punjab Police in 1995, was banned by the government two days after its release.

title: India-Australia Ties Enter New Phase With Uranium, Critical Mineral Exports
content: The centrepiece of the visit was a fresh push on uranium exports to India, an idea first floated under the 2015 civil nuclear agreement but which failed to gain real traction after an earlier attempt in 2010.

title: Headmaster Suspended After Vijay's Karur Speech Streamed Live In School
content: The Tamil Nadu government has suspended the headmistress of a Panchayat Union Middle School in Karur after she allegedly live-streamed Chief Minister Vijay's Karur event for students during class hours.

title: Cong demand for seat parity highlights fault line with SP
content: the new congress in charge for UP, Rajendra Pal Gautam, kickstarted his innings with a demand for parity and respect from the Samajwadi Party for the coming UP elections . it has raised concerns that such debate is distracting from serious issues to corner the governing BJP .

title: HC order on TMC bank a/cs may not affect ED freeze
content: ED is not a party to the petition on which the HC gave its judgment . the freeze on three of 18 TMC bank accounts by ED under section 17 of PMLA was not the matter being decided by the agency . a HC order relaxing the restraint imposed by Kolkata police on all accounts .

title: Won't be feasible to offer pure petrol, lower blends as options to E20: Govt
content: govt ruled out offering pure petrol, lower ethanol-petrol blends as options to E20 . it also said E20 offers a higher-octane rating, superior anti-knock characteristics, faster combustion, better pickup, smoother acceleration and cleaner engine operation . the petroleum ministry said maintaining parallel nationwide supply chains would complicate distribution across more than one lakh retail outlets .

title: Modi, first PM in NZ in 40 years, to capitalise on FTA, boost ties
content: the two sides are expected to announce initiatives for collaboration in high-performance sports to boost India's chances of hosting the Olympic Games in 2036 . both countries are currently celebrating 100 years of sporting ties and looking to translate the centenary partnership into tangible outcomes for athletes, coaches and sporting ecosystems .

title: Centre weighs Meta reply on WA usernames, rollout remains on hold
content: the ministry of electronics and information technology is assessing the feature's legal, privacy and cybersecurity implications . the proposed feature will allow users to create a unique identifier beginning with "@" and interact with others without disclosing their phone numbers .
